title: New Danish quantum software could accelerate future medicines
why:
- A new project called ODAQS will develop a software stack to make quantum computers easier to use in drug discovery and pharmaceutical research.
- The software aims to simplify and automate quantum programming, allowing users to work with quantum computers without needing to address each machine’s technical limitations.
- ODAQS will use reinforcement learning to optimize quantum software, making programs more efficient and maximizing the utility of current quantum hardware.
- Improved quantum software could reduce both the time and costs in the early stages of drug development by enabling more accurate chemical calculations.
- The project is a collaboration between Aarhus University, Aalborg University, and the quantum technology company Kvantify, supported by a DKK 17.4 million grant from Innovation Fund Denmark.
